BIPH898 Pre-Candidacy Research (1-8 Credits)

BIPH899 Doctoral Dissertation Research (1-8 Credits)

BISI - Biological Sciences

BISI712 Responsible Conduct of Research for Biologists (1 Credit) An exploration of the ethical dimensions associated with the responsible conduct of scientiﬁc research focusing on providing students with practical insights into the existing norms and expectations of working scientists. Credit Only Granted for: BISI712, BISI688B, or CBMG688B. Formerly: BISI688B and CBMG688B.

BISI610 Introduction to Behavior, Evolution, Ecology, & Systematics (2 Credits) This literature-based course serves as an intellectual orientation for new (i.e. ﬁrst- and second-year) doctoral students in the Behavior, Evolution, Ecology, and Systematics (BEES) concentration area of the Biological Sciences (BISI) Graduate Program. Restriction: Must be in the Behavior, Evolution, Ecology, and Systematics (BEES) concentration area of the Biological Sciences program; or permission of the instructor. Credit Only Granted for: BEES608A, BIOL608D or BISI610. Formerly: BEES608A.

BISI620 Bioinformatics and Genomics (2 Credits) Provides an overview of some major topics and research areas bioinformatics and genomics, and includes material from basic foundations through advanced concepts. Credit Only Granted for: CBMG688Y or BISI620. Formerly: CBMG688Y.
